entities.name: T.C.B.I.A. agreement

events.description: Chief of Branch of Realty H. M. Critchfield issued memorandum to Area Directors regarding requirement to cite applicable laws and approval dates for easements and rights of way in patent requests
Billings Area Office issued memorandum to all Superintendents regarding rights of way involved in issuance of patents in fee, attaching copy of December 13, 1954 office letter
Area Director issued memorandum to all Superintendents regarding issuance of patents and deeds subject to collection of unpaid irrigation assessments
Land Operations Conference held in Aberdeen Area, June 13-15, 1955, with committee appointments and reports on land operations
Committee appointed at Land Operations Conference to address reporting systems and conservation operations
Committee made preliminary report to the Land Operations Conference
Billings Area Office issued memorandum to all Superintendents attaching committee reports from Land Operations Conference held in Aberdeen Area
Transmittal to Superintendent, North Cheyenne, attaching copy of Office memorandum of 11/22/55 regarding fee patent policy
Central Office memorandum on fee patent policy set forth in memorandum of May 16 and subsequent policy statements
Billings Area Office memorandum on disposition of revenue from submarginal lands, quoting Central Office memorandum of November 23, 1955
Central Office memorandum on disposition of submarginal land revenues, directing funds to Treasury General Fund Receipt Account 1719
Federal Register Vol. 20, No. 155, page 5777 published amendment to 25 CFR 71.27 deleting reference to deposit of fees from submarginal land grazing privileges
Bureau memorandum issued regarding policy on issuance of patents in fee to competent applicants, generating numerous telegrams, letters, and postal cards in response
Billings Area Office transmitted fee patent policy statement to superintendents
Billings Area Office transmitted subsequent fee patent policy statement to superintendents

events.location: Pine Ridge, Rosebud, Crow Creek, Lower Brule Reservations
events.description: Range inventory/survey made by Soil Conservation Service personnel under T.C.B.I.A. agreement, now considered obsolete due to radical change in concept of scientific range inventory
Rex Carey, Agency Soil Scientist at Standing Rock, transferred to Pine Ridge with headquarters at Martin
Formal soil survey work plan being completed with SCS for Shannon County on Pine Ridge
BIA proposed to issue promptly a temporary manual release incorporating substance of May 16 memorandum and clarifying statement on fee patent policy

financial_transactions.description: Unpaid irrigation assessments and construction charges required to be collected per 25 CFR 151 and Act of March 7, 1928 before land sale transactions finalized; delinquent operation and maintenance charges must be deducted from land sale proceeds per bid form 5-114

Funds previously held in special deposits for submarginal land grazing privileges to be deposited to Treasury General Fund Receipt Account 1719, Rental of Land Not Otherwise Classified, following amendment to 25 CFR 71.27

financial_transactions.amount: 20% of lease rental or permit grazing fee annually
financial_transactions.description: Recommended annual investment in conservation improvements equivalent to 20% of lease rental or permit grazing fee as equitable investment for lessees on Indian lands

relationships.subject: Commissioner, Bureau of Indian Affairs

relationships.subject: Act of March 7, 1928

relationships.subject: 25 CFR 71.27
relationships.context: Regulation governing disposition of grazing fees from submarginal lands, amended August 10, 1955

correspondence.sender_title: Chief, Branch of Realty, Bureau of Indian Affairs
correspondence.recipient: Area Directors, Portland, Aberdeen, California, Billings, and Minneapolis
correspondence.subject: Request for issuance of patents in fee - requirement to cite applicable laws and approval dates for easements and rights of way
correspondence.action_requested: Specify applicable laws and approval dates for all easements and rights of way in patent requests to allow expeditious handling
correspondence.outcome: Forwarded to Billings Area superintendents on January 6, 1955

correspondence.subject: Rights of way involved in issuance of patents in fee
correspondence.action_requested: Obtain information on rights of way crossing restricted Indian lands and include in transmittals with patent applications or when Indian lands advertised for public sale
correspondence.outcome: Attached copy of December 13, 1954 office letter

correspondence.sender: Office of the Area Director, Billings
correspondence.recipient: All Superintendents and Officers in Charge
correspondence.subject: Issuance of patents and deeds subject to collection of unpaid irrigation assessments
correspondence.action_requested: Establish satisfactory checking procedure to ensure compliance with irrigation assessment requirements before land sale transactions are finalized
correspondence.outcome: Directive issued following Bureau auditors calling attention to failures to obtain payment for accrued charges

correspondence.subject: Issuance of patents in fee to competent applicants
correspondence.action_requested: All Bureau employees processing or reviewing fee patent applications to be guided by policy in May 16 memorandum and clarifying statement; exceptions to be returned to Superintendent for further action
correspondence.outcome: Temporary manual release to be issued incorporating substance of memorandum and statement

correspondence.recipient: All Superintendents and Officers in Charge, Billings Area
correspondence.subject: Disposition of revenue from submarginal lands
correspondence.action_requested: Deposit any funds held in special deposits from submarginal land grazing fees to Treasury General Fund Receipt Account 1719 before end of fiscal year
correspondence.outcome: Quoted Central Office memorandum of November 23, 1955

correspondence.sender: Billings Area Office, Branch of Realty
correspondence.subject: Fee patent policy as set forth in memorandum of May 16 and subsequent policy statements
correspondence.outcome: Attached copy of Office memorandum of 11/22/55

correspondence.subject: Committee reports, Land Operations Conference, Aberdeen Area
correspondence.action_requested: Provide comments on conclusions reached by several committees at the Land Operations Conference
correspondence.outcome: Attached committee reports from conference held at Sioux City, Iowa, June 13-15, 1955

correspondence.sender: Central Office, Bureau of Indian Affairs
correspondence.subject: Disposition of revenue from submarginal lands following amendment to 25 CFR 71.27
correspondence.action_requested: Deposit funds held in special deposits to Treasury; hereafter deposit revenues to appropriate general fund revenue receipt account
correspondence.outcome: Quoted in Billings Area Office memorandum of November 29, 1955

legislative_actions.outcome: Act of March 7, 1928 (45 Stat. 210) enacted, creating first lien against irrigable lands on Indian irrigation projects where construction, operation and maintenance costs remain unpaid, directing lien to be recited in any patent or instrument issued for such lands
Amendment to 25 CFR 71.27 published in Federal Register Vol. 20, No. 155, page 5777, deleting reference to deposit of fees from submarginal land grazing privileges in special deposits

legislative_actions.bill_number: legislation to transfer submarginal lands and fees to tribes
legislative_actions.outcome: Pending legislation to transfer submarginal lands and fees to respective tribes; special deposits previously held in support of this legislation; funds redirected to Treasury pending legislation
